}
div.unrecognized_cell .inner_cell a {
  color: inherit;
  text-decoration: none;
}
div.unrecognized_cell .inner_cell a:hover {
  color: inherit;
  text-decoration: none;
}
@media (max-width: 540px) {
  div.unrecognized_cell > div.prompt {
    display: none;
  }
}
div.code_cell {
  /* avoid page breaking on code cells when printing */
}
@media print {
  div.code_cell {
    page-break-inside: avoid;
  }
}
/* any special styling for code cells that are currently running goes here */
div.input {
  page-break-inside: avoid;
  /* Old browsers */
  display: -webkit-box;
  -webkit-box-orient: horizontal;
  -webkit-box-align: stretch;
  display: -moz-box;
  -moz-box-orient: horizontal;
  -moz-box-align: stretch;
  display: box;
  box-orient: horizontal;
  box-align: stretch;
  /* Modern browsers */
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: row;
  align-items: stretch;
}
@media (max-width: 540px) {
  div.input {
    /* Old browsers */
    display: -webkit-box;
    -webkit-box-orient: vertical;
    -webkit-box-align: stretch;
    display: -moz-box;
    -moz-box-orient: vertical;
    -moz-box-align: stretch;
    display: box;
    box-orient: vertical;
    box-align: stretch;
    /* Modern browsers */
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    align-items: stretch;
  }
}
/* input_area and input_prompt must match in top border and margin for alignment */
div.input_prompt {
  color: #303F9F;
  border-top: 1px solid transparent;
}
div.input_area > div.highlight {
  margin: 0.4em;
  border: none;
  padding: 0px;
  background-color: transparent;
}
div.input_area > div.highlight > pre {
  margin: 0px;
  border: none;
  padding: 0px;
  background-color: transparent;
}
/* The following gets added to the <head> if it is detected that the user has a
 * monospace font with inconsistent normal/bold/italic height.  See
 * notebookmain.js.  Such fonts will have keywords vertically offset with
 * respect to the rest of the text.  The user should select a better font.
 * See: https://github.com/ipython/ipython/issues/1503
 *
 * .CodeMirror span {
 *      vertical-align: bottom;
 * }
 */
.CodeMirror {
  line-height: 1.21429em;
  /* Changed from 1em to our global default */
  font-size: 14px;
  height: auto;
  /* Changed to auto to autogrow */
  background: none;
  /* Changed from white to allow our bg to show through */
}
.CodeMirror-scroll {
  /*  The CodeMirror docs are a bit fuzzy on if overflow-y should be hidden or visible.*/
  /*  We have found that if it is visible, vertical scrollbars appear with font size changes.*/
  overflow-y: hidden;
  overflow-x: auto;
}

  /* In CM2, this used to be 0.4em, but in CM3 it went to 4px. We need the em value because */
  /* we have set a different line-height and want this to scale with that. */

}

  padding: 0 8px 0 4px;
}
.CodeMirror-gutters {
  border-bottom-left-radius: 2px;
  border-top-left-radius: 2px;
}
.CodeMirror pre {
  /* In CM3 this went to 4px from 0 in CM2. We need the 0 value because of how we size */
  /* .CodeMirror-lines */
  padding: 0;
  border: 0;
  border-radius: 0;
}
/*

Adapted from GitHub theme

*/
.highlight-base {
  color: #000;
}
.highlight-variable {
  color: #000;
}
.highlight-variable-2 {
  color: #1a1a1a;
}

  color: #333333;
}

  color: #BA2121;
}

}

}

}
.highlight-keyword {
  color: #008000;
  font-weight: bold;
}
.highlight-builtin {
  color: #008000;
}
.highlight-error {
  color: #f00;
}
.highlight-operator {
  color: #AA22FF;
  font-weight: bold;

div.output_wrapper {
  /* this position must be relative to enable descendents to be absolute within it */
  position: relative;
  /* Old browsers */
  display: -webkit-box;
  -webkit-box-orient: vertical;
  -webkit-box-align: stretch;
  display: -moz-box;
  -moz-box-orient: vertical;
  -moz-box-align: stretch;
  display: box;
  box-orient: vertical;
  box-align: stretch;
  /* Modern browsers */
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  z-index: 1;
}
/* class for the output area when it should be height-limited */
div.output_scroll {
  /* ideally, this would be max-height, but FF barfs all over that */
  height: 24em;
  /* FF needs this *and the wrapper* to specify full width, or it will shrinkwrap */
  width: 100%;
  overflow: auto;
  border-radius: 2px;
  -webkit-box-shadow: inset 0 2px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8);
  box-shadow: inset 0 2px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8);
  display: block;
}
/* output div while it is collapsed */
div.output_collapsed {
  margin: 0px;
  padding: 0px;
  /* Old browsers */
  display: -webkit-box;
  -webkit-box-orient: vertical;
  -webkit-box-align: stretch;
  display: -moz-box;
  -moz-box-orient: vertical;
  -moz-box-align: stretch;
  display: box;
  box-orient: vertical;
  box-align: stretch;
  /* Modern browsers */
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
}
div.out_prompt_overlay {
  height: 100%;
  padding: 0px 0.4em;
  position: absolute;
  border-radius: 2px;
}
div.out_prompt_overlay:hover {
  /* use inner shadow to get border that is computed the same on WebKit/FF */
  -webkit-box-shadow: inset 0 0 1px #000;
  box-shadow: inset 0 0 1px #000;
  background: rgba(240, 240, 240, 0.5);
}
div.output_prompt {
  color: #D84315;
}
/* This class is the outer container of all output sections. */
div.output_area {
  padding: 0px;
  page-break-inside: avoid;
  /* Old browsers */
  display: -webkit-box;
  -webkit-box-orient: horizontal;
  -webkit-box-align: stretch;
  display: -moz-box;
  -moz-box-orient: horizontal;
  -moz-box-align: stretch;
  display: box;
  box-orient: horizontal;
  box-align: stretch;
  /* Modern browsers */
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: row;
  align-items: stretch;
}
div.output_area .MathJax_Display {
  text-align: left !important;
}
div.output_area 
div.output_area 
div.output_area img,
div.output_area svg {
  max-width: 100%;
  height: auto;
}
div.output_area img.unconfined,
div.output_area svg.unconfined {
  max-width: none;
}
/* This is needed to protect the pre formating from global settings such
   as that of bootstrap */
.output {
  /* Old browsers */
  display: -webkit-box;
  -webkit-box-orient: vertical;
  -webkit-box-align: stretch;
  display: -moz-box;
  -moz-box-orient: vertical;
  -moz-box-align: stretch;
  display: box;
  box-orient: vertical;
  box-align: stretch;
  /* Modern browsers */
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
}
@media (max-width: 540px) {
  div.output_area {
    /* Old browsers */
    display: -webkit-box;
    -webkit-box-orient: vertical;
    -webkit-box-align: stretch;
    display: -moz-box;
    -moz-box-orient: vertical;
    -moz-box-align: stretch;
    display: box;
    box-orient: vertical;
    box-align: stretch;
    /* Modern browsers */
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    align-items: stretch;
  }
}
div.output_area pre {
  margin: 0;
  padding: 0;
  border: 0;
  vertical-align: baseline;
  color: black;
  background-color: transparent;
  border-radius: 0;
}
/* This class is for the output subarea inside the output_area and after
   the prompt div. */
div.output_subarea {
  overflow-x: auto;
  padding: 0.4em;
  /* Old browsers */
  -webkit-box-flex: 1;
  -moz-box-flex: 1;
  box-flex: 1;
  /* Modern browsers */
  flex: 1;
  max-width: calc(100% - 14ex);
}
div.output_scroll div.output_subarea {
  overflow-x: visible;
}
/* The rest of the output_* classes are for special styling of the different
   output types */
/* all text output has this class: */
div.output_text {
  text-align: left;
  color: #000;
  /* This has to match that of the the CodeMirror class line-height below */
  line-height: 1.21429em;
}
/* stdout/stderr are 'text' as well as 'stream', but execute_result/error are *not* streams */
div.output_stderr {
  background: #fdd;
  /* very light red background for stderr */
}
div.output_latex {
  text-align: left;
}
/* Empty output_javascript divs should have no height */
div.output_javascript:empty {
  padding: 0;
}
.js-error {
  color: darkred;
}
/* raw_input styles */
div.raw_input_container {
  line-height: 1.21429em;
  padding-top: 5px;
}
pre.raw_input_prompt {

}
input.raw_input {
  font-family: monospace;
  font-size: inherit;
  color: inherit;
  width: auto;
  /* make sure input baseline aligns with prompt */

  /* padding + margin = 0.5em between prompt and cursor */

}

}

  text-decoration: none;
}

<div class="cell border-box-sizing text_cell rendered"><div class="prompt input_prompt">
</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="text_cell_render border-box-sizing rendered_html">
<p>We will look at various CSV files in the 'school' folder. Each one of these csv files contain information on NYC high schools. We will clean the datasets and condense it into one dataframe. The data cleaning process was done in the missions, so we'll quickly repeat the process in this notebook and focus on the data analysis.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing code_cell rendered">
<div class="input">
<div class="prompt input_prompt">In [1]:</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="input_area">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="nn">pandas</span>
<span class="kn">import</span> <span class="nn">numpy</span>
<span class="kn">import</span> <span class="nn">re</span>
<span class="kn">import</span> <span class="nn">matplotlib</span>

<span class="n">data_files</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="p">[</span>
    <span class="s2">"ap_2010.csv"</span><span class="p">,</span>
    <span class="s2">"class_size.csv"</span><span class="p">,</span>
    <span class="s2">"demographics.csv"</span><span class="p">,</span>
    <span class="s2">"graduation.csv"</span><span class="p">,</span>
    <span class="s2">"hs_directory.csv"</span><span class="p">,</span>
    <span class="s2">"sat_results.csv"</span>
<span class="p">]</span>

<span class="n">data</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="p">{}</span>

<span class="k">for</span> <span class="n">f</span> <span class="ow">in</span> <span class="n">data_files</span><span class="p">:</span>
    <span class="n">d</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">pandas</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">read_csv</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">"schools/</span><span class="si">{0}</span><span class="s2">"</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">format</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">f</span><span class="p">))</span>
    <span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">f</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">replace</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">".csv"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">""</span><span class="p">)]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">d</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing text_cell rendered"><div class="prompt input_prompt">
</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="text_cell_render border-box-sizing rendered_html">
<h3 id="Read-in-the-surveys">Read in the surveys<a class="anchor-link" href="#Read-in-the-surveys">¶</a></h3>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing code_cell rendered">
<div class="input">
<div class="prompt input_prompt">In [2]:</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="input_area">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="n">all_survey</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">pandas</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">read_csv</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">"schools/survey_all.txt"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">delimiter</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s2">"</span><span class="se">\t</span><span class="s2">"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">encoding</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'windows-1252'</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">d75_survey</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">pandas</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">read_csv</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">"schools/survey_d75.txt"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">delimiter</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s2">"</span><span class="se">\t</span><span class="s2">"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">encoding</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'windows-1252'</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">survey</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">pandas</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">concat</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="n">all_survey</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">d75_survey</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">axis</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="n">survey</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"DBN"</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">survey</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"dbn"</span><span class="p">]</span>

<span class="n">survey_fields</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="p">[</span>
    <span class="s2">"DBN"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"rr_s"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"rr_t"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"rr_p"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"N_s"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"N_t"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"N_p"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"saf_p_11"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"com_p_11"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"eng_p_11"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"aca_p_11"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"saf_t_11"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"com_t_11"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"eng_t_10"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"aca_t_11"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"saf_s_11"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"com_s_11"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"eng_s_11"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"aca_s_11"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"saf_tot_11"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"com_tot_11"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"eng_tot_11"</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="s2">"aca_tot_11"</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="p">]</span>
<span class="n">survey</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">survey</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">loc</span><span class="p">[:,</span><span class="n">survey_fields</span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"survey"</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">survey</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing text_cell rendered"><div class="prompt input_prompt">
</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="text_cell_render border-box-sizing rendered_html">
<h3 id="Add-DBN-columns">Add DBN columns<a class="anchor-link" href="#Add-DBN-columns">¶</a></h3>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing code_cell rendered">
<div class="input">
<div class="prompt input_prompt">In [3]:</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="input_area">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"hs_directory"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="s2">"DBN"</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"hs_directory"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="s2">"dbn"</span><span class="p">]</span>

<span class="k">def</span> <span class="nf">pad_csd</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">num</span><span class="p">):</span>
    <span class="n">string_representation</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="nb">str</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">num</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="nb">len</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">string_representation</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="o">></span> <span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">:</span>
        <span class="k">return</span> <span class="n">string_representation</span>
    <span class="k">else</span><span class="p">:</span>
        <span class="k">return</span> <span class="s2">"0"</span> <span class="o">+</span> <span class="n">string_representation</span>
    
<span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"class_size"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="s2">"padded_csd"</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"class_size"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="s2">"CSD"</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">apply</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">pad_csd</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"class_size"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="s2">"DBN"</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"class_size"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="s2">"padded_csd"</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">+</span> <span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"class_size"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="s2">"SCHOOL CODE"</span><span class="p">]</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing text_cell rendered"><div class="prompt input_prompt">
</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="text_cell_render border-box-sizing rendered_html">
<h3 id="Convert-columns-to-numeric">Convert columns to numeric<a class="anchor-link" href="#Convert-columns-to-numeric">¶</a></h3>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing code_cell rendered">
<div class="input">
<div class="prompt input_prompt">In [4]:</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="input_area">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="n">cols</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'SAT Math Avg. Score'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">'SAT Critical Reading Avg. Score'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">'SAT Writing Avg. Score'</span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="k">for</span> <span class="n">c</span> <span class="ow">in</span> <span class="n">cols</span><span class="p">:</span>
    <span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"sat_results"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="n">c</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">pandas</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">to_numeric</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"sat_results"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="n">c</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">errors</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s2">"coerce"</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'sat_results'</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="s1">'sat_score'</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'sat_results'</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="n">cols</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">]]</span> <span class="o">+</span> <span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'sat_results'</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="n">cols</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">]]</span> <span class="o">+</span> <span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'sat_results'</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="n">cols</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">]]</span>

<span class="k">def</span> <span class="nf">find_lat</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">loc</span><span class="p">):</span>
    <span class="n">coords</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">re</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">findall</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">"\(.+, .+\)"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">loc</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="n">lat</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">coords</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">split</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">","</span><span class="p">)[</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">replace</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">"("</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">""</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="k">return</span> <span class="n">lat</span>

<span class="k">def</span> <span class="nf">find_lon</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">loc</span><span class="p">):</span>
    <span class="n">coords</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">re</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">findall</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">"\(.+, .+\)"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">loc</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="n">lon</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">coords</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">split</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">","</span><span class="p">)[</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">replace</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">")"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">""</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">strip</span><span class="p">()</span>
    <span class="k">return</span> <span class="n">lon</span>

<span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"hs_directory"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="s2">"lat"</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"hs_directory"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="s2">"Location 1"</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">apply</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">find_lat</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"hs_directory"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="s2">"lon"</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"hs_directory"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="s2">"Location 1"</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">apply</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">find_lon</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"hs_directory"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="s2">"lat"</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">pandas</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">to_numeric</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"hs_directory"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="s2">"lat"</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">errors</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s2">"coerce"</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"hs_directory"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="s2">"lon"</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">pandas</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">to_numeric</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"hs_directory"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="s2">"lon"</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">errors</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s2">"coerce"</span><span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing text_cell rendered"><div class="prompt input_prompt">
</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="text_cell_render border-box-sizing rendered_html">
<h3 id="Condense-datasets">Condense datasets<a class="anchor-link" href="#Condense-datasets">¶</a></h3>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing code_cell rendered">
<div class="input">
<div class="prompt input_prompt">In [5]:</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="input_area">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="n">class_size</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"class_size"</span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="n">class_size</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">class_size</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">class_size</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"GRADE "</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">==</span> <span class="s2">"09-12"</span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="n">class_size</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">class_size</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">class_size</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"PROGRAM TYPE"</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">==</span> <span class="s2">"GEN ED"</span><span class="p">]</span>

<span class="n">class_size</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">class_size</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">groupby</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">"DBN"</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">agg</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">numpy</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">mean</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">class_size</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">reset_index</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">inplace</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">True</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"class_size"</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">class_size</span>

<span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"demographics"</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"demographics"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"demographics"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="s2">"schoolyear"</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">==</span> <span class="mi">20112012</span><span class="p">]</span>

<span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"graduation"</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"graduation"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"graduation"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="s2">"Cohort"</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">==</span> <span class="s2">"2006"</span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"graduation"</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"graduation"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"graduation"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="s2">"Demographic"</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">==</span> <span class="s2">"Total Cohort"</span><span class="p">]</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing text_cell rendered"><div class="prompt input_prompt">
</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="text_cell_render border-box-sizing rendered_html">
<h3 id="Convert-AP-scores-to-numeric">Convert AP scores to numeric<a class="anchor-link" href="#Convert-AP-scores-to-numeric">¶</a></h3>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing code_cell rendered">
<div class="input">
<div class="prompt input_prompt">In [6]:</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="input_area">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="n">cols</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'AP Test Takers '</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">'Total Exams Taken'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">'Number of Exams with scores 3 4 or 5'</span><span class="p">]</span>

<span class="k">for</span> <span class="n">col</span> <span class="ow">in</span> <span class="n">cols</span><span class="p">:</span>
    <span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"ap_2010"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="n">col</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">pandas</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">to_numeric</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"ap_2010"</span><span class="p">][</span><span class="n">col</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">errors</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s2">"coerce"</span><span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing text_cell rendered"><div class="prompt input_prompt">
</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="text_cell_render border-box-sizing rendered_html">
<h3 id="Combine-the-datasets">Combine the datasets<a class="anchor-link" href="#Combine-the-datasets">¶</a></h3>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing code_cell rendered">
<div class="input">
<div class="prompt input_prompt">In [7]:</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="input_area">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="n">combined</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"sat_results"</span><span class="p">]</span>

<span class="n">combined</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">combined</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">merge</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"ap_2010"</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">on</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s2">"DBN"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">how</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s2">"left"</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">combined</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">combined</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">merge</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"graduation"</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">on</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s2">"DBN"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">how</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s2">"left"</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="n">to_merge</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"class_size"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">"demographics"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">"survey"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">"hs_directory"</span><span class="p">]</span>

<span class="k">for</span> <span class="n">m</span> <span class="ow">in</span> <span class="n">to_merge</span><span class="p">:</span>
    <span class="n">combined</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">combined</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">merge</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">data</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">m</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">on</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s2">"DBN"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">how</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s2">"inner"</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="n">combined</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">combined</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">fillna</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">combined</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">mean</span><span class="p">())</span>
<span class="n">combined</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">combined</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">fillna</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing text_cell rendered"><div class="prompt input_prompt">
</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="text_cell_render border-box-sizing rendered_html">
<h3 id="Add-a-school-district-column-for-mapping">Add a school district column for mapping<a class="anchor-link" href="#Add-a-school-district-column-for-mapping">¶</a></h3>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing code_cell rendered">
<div class="input">
<div class="prompt input_prompt">In [8]:</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="input_area">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="k">def</span> <span class="nf">get_first_two_chars</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">dbn</span><span class="p">):</span>
    <span class="k">return</span> <span class="n">dbn</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">]</span>

<span class="n">combined</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"school_dist"</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">combined</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"DBN"</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">apply</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">get_first_two_chars</span><span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing text_cell rendered"><div class="prompt input_prompt">
</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="text_cell_render border-box-sizing rendered_html">
<h3 id="Find-correlations">Find correlations<a class="anchor-link" href="#Find-correlations">¶</a></h3>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing code_cell rendered">
<div class="input">
<div class="prompt input_prompt">In [9]:</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="input_area">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="n">correlations</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">combined</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">corr</span><span class="p">()</span>
<span class="n">correlations</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">correlations</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"sat_score"</span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">correlations</span><span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<p>We are interested the correlations with the columns specified in survey_fields.</p>

<div class="cell border-box-sizing text_cell rendered"><div class="prompt input_prompt">
</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="text_cell_render border-box-sizing rendered_html">
<p>The saf_s_11 column measures how students perceive safety at the school, let's see how students do in the SATs if the schools have higher safety scores.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing code_cell rendered">
<div class="input">
<div class="prompt input_prompt">In [11]:</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="input_area">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="n">combined</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">plot</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">scatter</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="s1">'sat_score'</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"saf_s_11"</span><span class="p">])</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="cell border-box-sizing text_cell rendered"><div class="prompt input_prompt">
</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="text_cell_render border-box-sizing rendered_html">
<p>Looks like there is a weak positive correlation between safety and SAT scores. Maybe it is easier for students to study when they are not worried about safety.</p>
<p>We can generate a NYC basemap and see which schools have lower safety scores, and color label the schools with lower safety scores with a different color</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing code_cell rendered">
<div class="input">
<div class="prompt input_prompt">In [12]:</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="input_area">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="nn">matplotlib.pyplot</span> <span class="k">as</span> <span class="nn">plt</span>
<span class="kn">from</span> <span class="nn">mpl_toolkits.basemap</span> <span class="k">import</span> <span class="n">Basemap</span>

<span class="n">districts</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">combined</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">groupby</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">"school_dist"</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">agg</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">numpy</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">mean</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">districts</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">reset_index</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">inplace</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">True</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="n">m</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">Basemap</span><span class="p">(</span>
    <span class="n">projection</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'merc'</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="n">llcrnrlat</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mf">40.496044</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="n">urcrnrlat</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mf">40.915256</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="n">llcrnrlon</span><span class="o">=-</span><span class="mf">74.255735</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="n">urcrnrlon</span><span class="o">=-</span><span class="mf">73.700272</span><span class="p">,</span>
    <span class="n">resolution</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'i'</span>
<span class="p">)</span>

<span class="n">m</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">drawmapboundary</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">fill_color</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'#85A6D9'</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">m</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">drawcoastlines</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">color</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'#6D5F47'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">linewidth</span><span class="o">=.</span><span class="mi">4</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">m</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">drawrivers</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">color</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'#6D5F47'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">linewidth</span><span class="o">=.</span><span class="mi">4</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1">#converts to list</span>
<span class="n">longitudes</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">districts</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'lon'</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tolist</span><span class="p">()</span>
<span class="n">latitudes</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">districts</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'lat'</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tolist</span><span class="p">()</span>
<span class="c1">#generates a scatterplot with the 2 lists as arguments, s=20 for size of points, zorder=2 for dots on top of the map, latlong=True to indicate we are putting in the latitude/longitude</span>
<span class="n">m</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">scatter</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">longitudes</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">latitudes</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">s</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">50</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">zorder</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">latlon</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">True</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">c</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">districts</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'saf_s_11'</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">cmap</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'summer'</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">plt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">show</span><span class="p">()</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="cell border-box-sizing text_cell rendered"><div class="prompt input_prompt">
</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="text_cell_render border-box-sizing rendered_html">
<p>Next we are interested to see if there is a correlation between race and SAT scores.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing code_cell rendered">
<div class="input">
<div class="prompt input_prompt">In [14]:</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="input_area">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="n">races</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="p">[</span>
    <span class="s1">'white_per'</span><span class="p">,</span>
    <span class="s1">'asian_per'</span><span class="p">,</span>
    <span class="s1">'black_per'</span><span class="p">,</span>
    <span class="s1">'hispanic_per'</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="p">]</span>

<span class="n">correlations</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">races</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">plot</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">bar</span><span class="p">()</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="cell border-box-sizing text_cell rendered"><div class="prompt input_prompt">
</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="text_cell_render border-box-sizing rendered_html">
<p>We are seeing positive correlation for whites/asians in SAT scores, negative correlations for blacks/hispanic in SAT scores. However, I wouldn't say any of these correlations are very strong. Let's use a scatter plot for hispanic to see if we can draw any insights.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing code_cell rendered">
<div class="input">
<div class="prompt input_prompt">In [15]:</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="input_area">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="n">combined</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">plot</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">scatter</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="s1">'sat_score'</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">"hispanic_per"</span><span class="p">])</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="cell border-box-sizing text_cell rendered"><div class="prompt input_prompt">
</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="text_cell_render border-box-sizing rendered_html">
<p>Schools with high amount of hispanics do poorly in the SATs. Schools with low amount of hispanics can do relatively well in the SATs. Let's filter the data down to scools with >95% Hispanics</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<span class="n">schools_with_hispanics</span>

</div>

<div class="cell border-box-sizing text_cell rendered"><div class="prompt input_prompt">
</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="text_cell_render border-box-sizing rendered_html">
<p>Using google searches, we found out that these schools have students who recently immigrated, so most of them are probably still ESL students. It makes sense they do not perform as well in SATs.</p>
<p>Next we'll look correlations between gender and SAT scores</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing code_cell rendered">
<div class="input">
<div class="prompt input_prompt">In [18]:</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="input_area">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="n">genders</span> <span class="o">=</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'male_per'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">'female_per'</span><span class="p">]</span>

<span class="n">correlations</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">genders</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">plot</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">bar</span><span class="p">()</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="output_wrapper">
<div class="output">
<div class="output_area">
<div class="prompt output_prompt">Out[18]:</div>
<div class="output_text output_subarea output_execute_result">

</div>
</div>

<p>Seems like theres very little correlation overall for both sides small positive correlation for females and small negative correlation for males.</p>

<div class="cell border-box-sizing text_cell rendered"><div class="prompt input_prompt">
</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="text_cell_render border-box-sizing rendered_html">
<p>Scatter plot doesn't show much, data seems very scattered and we are not seeing much correlation between schools with more females and SAT scores.</p>
<p>Let's look at schools with < 60% females and higher than 1700 SAT scores</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing code_cell rendered">
<div class="input">
<div class="prompt input_prompt">In [20]:</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="input_area">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="n">schools_with_hf</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">combined</span><span class="p">[(</span><span class="n">combined</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'female_per'</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o"><</span> <span class="mi">60</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="o">&</span> 
                           <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">combined</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'sat_score'</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">></span> <span class="mi">1700</span><span class="p">)]</span>
<span class="n">schools_with_hf</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="cell border-box-sizing text_cell rendered"><div class="prompt input_prompt">
</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="text_cell_render border-box-sizing rendered_html">
<p>A google search shows that these schools are heavily STEM focused and have higher entrance requirements, so it makes sense that  they have higher SAT scores</p>
<p>Let's take a look at AP test takers in these schools. It is possible that AP test takers score higher in SATS.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing code_cell rendered">
<div class="input">
<div class="prompt input_prompt">In [21]:</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="input_area">
<div class=" highlight hl-ipython3"><pre><span></span><span class="n">combined</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'ap_per'</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">combined</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'AP Test Takers '</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">/</span><span class="n">combined</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'total_enrollment'</span><span class="p">]</span>

<span class="n">combined</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">plot</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">scatter</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="s1">'ap_per'</span><span class="p">],[</span><span class="s1">'sat_score'</span><span class="p">])</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="cell border-box-sizing text_cell rendered"><div class="prompt input_prompt">
</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="text_cell_render border-box-sizing rendered_html">
<p>Schools with low AP test takers tend to score low on SAT as well. Wheras schools with high AP test takers generally score higher. This makes sense because students who enroll in AP classes are generally honor students so they study hard for the SATs as well.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="cell border-box-sizing text_cell rendered"><div class="prompt input_prompt">
</div>
<div class="inner_cell">
<div class="text_cell_render border-box-sizing rendered_html">
<hr />
<h4 id="Learning-Summary">Learning Summary<a class="anchor-link" href="#Learning-Summary">¶</a></h4><p>Python concepts explored: pandas, matplotlib.pyplot, correlations, regex, basemap, data analysis, string manipulation</p>
<p>Python functions and methods used: .scatter(), info(), .tolist(), .groupby(), .agg(), .concat(), .apply(), .strip, .merge(), .fillna(), .corr()</p>
<p>The files used for this project can be found in my <a href="https://github.com/sengkchu/Dataquest-Guided-Projects-Solutions/tree/master/Guided%20Project_%20Analyzing%20NYC%20High%20School%20Data">GitHub repository</a>.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>

entry.title: Visualizing The Gender Gap In College Degrees
entry.summary: First, I would like to point out that this project has nothing to do with the gender wage gap. We are simply going to look at the number of women in various majors between 1968-2011. We will then plot our findings to see if there are majors with less women than others. We'll focus on plotting asthetics in this project.
entry.content:
<style type="text/css">/*!

*/
/* CSS font colors for translated ANSI colors. */
.ansibold {
  font-weight: bold;
}
/* use dark versions for foreground, to improve visibility */

}

  color: darkred;
}

  color: darkgreen;
}
.ansiyellow {

}
.ansiblue {
  color: darkblue;
}
.ansipurple {
  color: darkviolet;
}
.ansicyan {
  color: steelblue;
}
.ansigray {
  color: gray;
}
/* and light for background, for the same reason */
.ansibgblack {
  background-color: black;
}
.ansibgred {
  background-color: red;
}
.ansibggreen {
  background-color: green;
}
.ansibgyellow {
  background-color: yellow;
}
.ansibgblue {
  background-color: blue;
}
.ansibgpurple {
  background-color: magenta;
}
.ansibgcyan {
  background-color: cyan;
}
.ansibggray {
  background-color: gray;
}
div.cell {
  /* Old browsers */
  display: -webkit-box;
  -webkit-box-orient: vertical;
  -webkit-box-align: stretch;
  display: -moz-box;
  -moz-box-orient: vertical;
  -moz-box-align: stretch;
  display: box;
  box-orient: vertical;
  box-align: stretch;
  /* Modern browsers */
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  border-radius: 2px;
  box-sizing: border-box;
  -moz-box-sizing: border-box;
  -webkit-box-sizing: border-box;
  border-width: 1px;
  border-style: solid;
  border-color: transparent;
  width: 100%;
  padding: 5px;
  /* This acts as a spacer between cells, that is outside the border */
  margin: 0px;
  outline: none;
  border-left-width: 1px;
  padding-left: 5px;
  background: linear-gradient(to right, transparent -40px, transparent 1px, transparent 1px, transparent 100%);
}
div.cell.jupyter-soft-selected {
  border-left-color: #90CAF9;
  border-left-color: #E3F2FD;
  border-left-width: 1px;
  padding-left: 5px;
  border-right-color: #E3F2FD;
  border-right-width: 1px;
  background: #E3F2FD;
}
@media print {
  div.cell.jupyter-soft-selected {
    border-color: transparent;
  }
}
div.cell.selected {
  border-color: #ababab;
  border-left-width: 0px;
  padding-left: 6px;
  background: linear-gradient(to right, #42A5F5 -40px, #42A5F5 5px, transparent 5px, transparent 100%);
}
@media print {
  div.cell.selected {
    border-color: transparent;
  }
}
div.cell.selected.jupyter-soft-selected {
  border-left-width: 0;
  padding-left: 6px;
  background: linear-gradient(to right, #42A5F5 -40px, #42A5F5 7px, #E3F2FD 7px, #E3F2FD 100%);
}
.edit_mode div.cell.selected {
  border-color: #66BB6A;
  border-left-width: 0px;
  padding-left: 6px;
  background: linear-gradient(to right, #66BB6A -40px, #66BB6A 5px, transparent 5px, transparent 100%);
}
@media print {
  .edit_mode div.cell.selected {
    border-color: transparent;
  }
}
.prompt {
  /* This needs to be wide enough for 3 digit prompt numbers: In[100]: */
  min-width: 14ex;
  /* This padding is tuned to match the padding on the CodeMirror editor. */
  padding: 0.4em;
  margin: 0px;
  font-family: monospace;
  text-align: right;
  /* This has to match that of the the CodeMirror class line-height below */
  line-height: 1.21429em;
  /* Don't highlight prompt number selection */
  -webkit-touch-callout: none;
  -webkit-user-select: none;
  -khtml-user-select: none;
  -moz-user-select: none;
  -ms-user-select: none;
  user-select: none;
  /* Use default cursor */
  cursor: default;
}
@media (max-width: 540px) {
  .prompt {
    text-align: left;
  }
}
div.inner_cell {
  min-width: 0;
  /* Old browsers */
  display: -webkit-box;
  -webkit-box-orient: vertical;
  -webkit-box-align: stretch;
  display: -moz-box;
  -moz-box-orient: vertical;
  -moz-box-align: stretch;
  display: box;
  box-orient: vertical;
  box-align: stretch;
  /* Modern browsers */
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  /* Old browsers */
  -webkit-box-flex: 1;
  -moz-box-flex: 1;
  box-flex: 1;
  /* Modern browsers */
  flex: 1;
}
/* input_area and input_prompt must match in top border and margin for alignment */
div.input_area {
  border: 1px solid #cfcfcf;
  border-radius: 2px;
  background: #f7f7f7;
  line-height: 1.21429em;
}
/* This is needed so that empty prompt areas can collapse to zero height when there
   is no content in the output_subarea and the prompt. The main purpose of this is
   to make sure that empty JavaScript output_subareas have no height. */
div.prompt:empty {
  padding-top: 0;
  padding-bottom: 0;
}
div.unrecognized_cell {
  padding: 5px 5px 5px 0px;
  /* Old browsers */
  display: -webkit-box;
  -webkit-box-orient: horizontal;
  -webkit-box-align: stretch;
  display: -moz-box;
  -moz-box-orient: horizontal;
  -moz-box-align: stretch;
  display: box;
  box-orient: horizontal;
  box-align: stretch;
  /* Modern browsers */
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: row;
  align-items: stretch;
}
div.unrecognized_cell .inner_cell {
  border-radius: 2px;
  padding: 5px;
  font-weight: bold;
  color: red;
  border: 1px solid #cfcfcf;
  background: #eaeaea;
}
div.unrecognized_cell .inner_cell a {
  color: inherit;
  text-decoration: none;
}
div.unrecognized_cell .inner_cell a:hover {
  color: inherit;
  text-decoration: none;
}
@media (max-width: 540px) {
  div.unrecognized_cell > div.prompt {
    display: none;
  }
}
div.code_cell {
  /* avoid page breaking on code cells when printing */
}
@media print {
  div.code_cell {
    page-break-inside: avoid;
  }
}
/* any special styling for code cells that are currently running goes here */
div.input {
  page-break-inside: avoid;
  /* Old browsers */
  display: -webkit-box;
  -webkit-box-orient: horizontal;
  -webkit-box-align: stretch;
  display: -moz-box;
  -moz-box-orient: horizontal;
  -moz-box-align: stretch;
  display: box;
  box-orient: horizontal;
  box-align: stretch;
  /* Modern browsers */
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: row;
  align-items: stretch;
}
@media (max-width: 540px) {
  div.input {
    /* Old browsers */
    display: -webkit-box;
    -webkit-box-orient: vertical;
    -webkit-box-align: stretch;
    display: -moz-box;
    -moz-box-orient: vertical;
    -moz-box-align: stretch;
    display: box;
    box-orient: vertical;
    box-align: stretch;
    /* Modern browsers */
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    align-items: stretch;
  }
}
/* input_area and input_prompt must match in top border and margin for alignment */
div.input_prompt {
  color: #303F9F;
  border-top: 1px solid transparent;
}
div.input_area > div.highlight {
  margin: 0.4em;
  border: none;
  padding: 0px;
  background-color: transparent;
}
div.input_area > div.highlight > pre {
  margin: 0px;
  border: none;
  padding: 0px;
  background-color: transparent;
}
/* The following gets added to the <head> if it is detected that the user has a
 * monospace font with inconsistent normal/bold/italic height.  See
 * notebookmain.js.  Such fonts will have keywords vertically offset with
 * respect to the rest of the text.  The user should select a better font.
 * See: https://github.com/ipython/ipython/issues/1503
 *
 * .CodeMirror span {
 *      vertical-align: bottom;
 * }
 */
.CodeMirror {
  line-height: 1.21429em;
  /* Changed from 1em to our global default */
  font-size: 14px;
  height: auto;
  /* Changed to auto to autogrow */
  background: none;
  /* Changed from white to allow our bg to show through */
}
.CodeMirror-scroll {
  /*  The CodeMirror docs are a bit fuzzy on if overflow-y should be hidden or visible.*/
  /*  We have found that if it is visible, vertical scrollbars appear with font size changes.*/
  overflow-y: hidden;
  overflow-x: auto;
}
.CodeMirror-lines {
  /* In CM2, this used to be 0.4em, but in CM3 it went to 4px. We need the em value because */
  /* we have set a different line-height and want this to scale with that. */
  padding: 0.4em;
}
.CodeMirror-linenumber {
  padding: 0 8px 0 4px;
}
.CodeMirror-gutters {
  border-bottom-left-radius: 2px;
  border-top-left-radius: 2px;
}
.CodeMirror pre {
  /* In CM3 this went to 4px from 0 in CM2. We need the 0 value because of how we size */
  /* .CodeMirror-lines */
  padding: 0;
  border: 0;
  border-radius: 0;
}

div.output_wrapper {
  /* this position must be relative to enable descendents to be absolute within it */
  position: relative;
  /* Old browsers */
  display: -webkit-box;
  -webkit-box-orient: vertical;
  -webkit-box-align: stretch;
  display: -moz-box;
  -moz-box-orient: vertical;
  -moz-box-align: stretch;
  display: box;
  box-orient: vertical;
  box-align: stretch;
  /* Modern browsers */
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  z-index: 1;
}
/* class for the output area when it should be height-limited */
div.output_scroll {
  /* ideally, this would be max-height, but FF barfs all over that */
  height: 24em;
  /* FF needs this *and the wrapper* to specify full width, or it will shrinkwrap */
  width: 100%;
  overflow: auto;
  border-radius: 2px;
  -webkit-box-shadow: inset 0 2px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8);
  box-shadow: inset 0 2px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8);
  display: block;
}
/* output div while it is collapsed */
div.output_collapsed {
  margin: 0px;
  padding: 0px;
  /* Old browsers */

  -webkit-box-orient: vertical;

  flex-direction: column;

}
div.out_prompt_overlay {
  height: 100%;
  padding: 0px 0.4em;
  position: absolute;
  border-radius: 2px;
}
div.out_prompt_overlay:hover {
  /* use inner shadow to get border that is computed the same on WebKit/FF */

  box-shadow: inset 0 0 1px #000;
  background: rgba(240, 240, 240, 0.5);
}

/* This class is the outer container of all output sections. */

.output {

  display: -moz-box;

}
div.output_area pre {
  margin: 0;
  padding: 0;
  border: 0;
  vertical-align: baseline;
  color: black;
  background-color: transparent;
  border-radius: 0;
}
/* This class is for the output subarea inside the output_area and after
   the prompt div. */
div.output_subarea {
  overflow-x: auto;
  padding: 0.4em;
  /* Old browsers */
  -webkit-box-flex: 1;
  -moz-box-flex: 1;
  box-flex: 1;
  /* Modern browsers */
  flex: 1;
  max-width: calc(100% - 14ex);
}
div.output_scroll div.output_subarea {
  overflow-x: visible;
}
/* The rest of the output_* classes are for special styling of the different
   output types */
/* all text output has this class: */
div.output_text {
  text-align: left;
  color: #000;
  /* This has to match that of the the CodeMirror class line-height below */
  line-height: 1.21429em;
}
/* stdout/stderr are 'text' as well as 'stream', but execute_result/error are *not* streams */
div.output_stderr {
  background: #fdd;
  /* very light red background for stderr */
}
div.output_latex {
  text-align: left;
}
/* Empty output_javascript divs should have no height */

/* raw_input styles */
